question.text: Under SEBI (ICDR) Regulations 2018, what is the minimum subscription to be received in a public issue as a percentage of the offer through the offer document?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 45(1) states that the minimum subscription to be received in an issue shall be at least ninety per cent (90%) of the offer through offer document, except in case of an offer for sale of specified securities. **The Trap:** 75% is no longer the threshold. The current ICDR Regulations have set it at 90%, which is higher to ensure that the issue is substantially subscribed before proceeding. **Cross-Exam Context:** This 90% minimum subscription requirement is tested across NISM exams dealing with primary market regulations. The same 90% applies to rights issues under Regulation 86(1). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If minimum subscription is not received, all application moneys must be refunded within 4 days of the closure of the issue. For rights issues financing non-capex purposes, the 90% requirement may be relaxed if promoters fully subscribe.
